support fp8_marlin via compressed-tensors

testing should be covered by existing models running on Ampere, but also added a weight-only FP8 checkpoint

Evals on GSM8k for per-tensor and channelwise checkpoints:
vllm (pretrained=neuralmagic/

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix `kv_cache_dtype=fp8` without scales for FP8 checkpoints
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #6738
Enabling kv_cache_dtype=fp8 quantization without scales in an FP8 model checkpoint was broken since the default k/v_scale wasn't saved as a float.
On main you would see this error if enabling fp8 kv cache with an FP8 model:
> vllm serve neuralmagic/Qwen2-0.5B-Instruct-FP8 --enforce-eager -

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix empty (nullptr) channelwise  scales when loading wNa16 using compressed tensors
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When running row parallel with channelwise scales, the integer divide in:
// in vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/compressed_tensors/schemes/compressed_tensors_wNa16.py:
scales_and_zp_size = input_size_per_partition // group_size

would result in a 0 sized tensor (with shape (0, 8192), i.e. te

vllm.title: [Core] Fix ray forward_dag error mssg
vllm.author: rkooo567
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This error msg was not a real issue from the CI. But it happens as a side effect of other unexpected failure.
It happens because self.forward_dag is defined after init_workers_ray. If init_workers_ray has an exception. self.forward_dag doesn't exist, so it will raise the following exception.

vllm.author: eaplatanios
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This was broken in the 0.5.3 release when these signal calls were introduced. It results in the following error when deploying on our machines:
ValueError: signal only works in main thread of the main interpreter

The fix is borrowed from here.
It would be great if you could cut a 0.5.3.post2 releas

vllm.title: [Core] Use array to speedup padding
vllm.author: peng1999
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Using array.array in SequenceData greatly improves performance of make_tensor_with_pad in Sampler.
Micro-benchmark using 1024 input length and 2048 batch size shows a great latency improvment (79ms to 22ms):
Before:

After:

End-to-end test on qwen-1.5-0.5b model also shows improvement on throughput
